title: lysimachia caloneura
intro: Lysimachia caloneura, also known as the golden creeping Jenny or Lysimachia nummularia 'Aurea', is a type of flowering plant in the primrose family, characterized by its golden-yellow leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Lysimachia caloneura include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for cardiovascular health and skin conditions. Lysimachia caloneura can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
